title: Date formula not working
excerpt: New user, pulling my hair out here.  I’m trying to write an extremely simple column formula to calculate whether a product is out of warranty.  It looks at a “warranty good through” date field and compares it to TODAY, s…

posts.author: BillReid (Bill Reid)
posts.cookedHtml:
New user, pulling my hair out here. I’m trying to write an extremely simple column formula to calculate whether a product is out of warranty. It looks at a “warranty good through” date field and compares it to TODAY, showing whether a warranty is Active or Expired.

IF({Warranty Exp}<TODAY(),“Active”,“Expired”)

This returns “Expired” for every solution.

posts.author: BillReid (Bill Reid)
posts.cookedHtml: It will, however, return “Active” if {Warranty Exp} is blank?

posts.author: LKat (Laura Katterhenry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Got it! You have to use both the IF and DAYS formulas:
IF(DAYS({Warranty Exp},TODAY())<0,“Active”,“Expired”)

posts.author: LKat (Laura Katterhenry)
posts.cookedHtml: DAYS will compare the number of days between two dates.

posts.author: BillReid (Bill Reid)
posts.cookedHtml: If I use that, it returns “Illegal Formula” ?

posts.author: BillReid (Bill Reid)
posts.cookedHtml:
Nevermind that - just had to include some spacing in the formula. Working fine!

Thanks!
